questions.question: What is the time complexity of the brute force approach to solve the Traveling Salesman Problem using exhaustive search?
questions.hint: The number of possible permutations of n cities is 2^n. Therefore, the time complexity is O(2^n).

questions.question: What is the main disadvantage of the brute force approach to solve the Traveling Salesman Problem using exhaustive search?
questions.hint: The time complexity of the brute force approach is very high, which makes it impractical for large number of cities.

questions.question: How can the brute force approach to solve the Traveling Salesman Problem using exhaustive search be optimized?
questions.hint: One way to optimize the brute force approach is to use symmetry properties of the TSP, such as mirror image or rotational symmetry, to reduce the number of permutations to be evaluated.
